perturbation.original_text: Contribution by Sponsor: In consideration for the right to sponsor the Kaya Fest and to be acknowledged by Fruit of Life Productions LLC., as a Promoter of the event during the term of this Agreement, Sponsor agrees to contract with Fruit of Life Productions LLC., for the amount of $250,000.00 to be paid in full upon signing of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Contribution by Sponsor: In consideration for the right to sponsor the Kaya Fest and to be acknowledged by Fruit of Life Productions LLC., as a Promoter of the event during the term of this Agreement, Sponsor agrees to contract with Fruit of Life Productions LLC., for the amount of $250,000.00 to be paid in 30 days after signing of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the payment deadline. Initially, the agreement stated the payment of $250,000.00 was 'to be paid in full upon signing of this agreement.' The modification states it's 'to be paid in 30 days after signing of this agreement.' This creates uncertainty regarding when the payment is actually due, as it contradicts the initial immediate payment condition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SPONSORSHIP AGREEMENT This agreement (the "Agreement") is made effective this 1st day of April, 2018 ("Effective Date") between Fruit of Life Productions LLC, ("Promoter") and Eco Science Solutions, Inc.,("Sponsor"), 1135 Makawao Avenue, Suite 103-188, Makawao, Hawaii, 96768. Contribution by Sponsor: In consideration for the right to sponsor the Kaya Fest and to be acknowledged by Fruit of Life Productions LLC., as a Promoter of the event during the term of this Agreement, Sponsor agrees to contract with Fruit of Life Productions LLC., for the amount of $250,000.00 to be paid in 30 days after signing of this agreement. Bank Wire Transformation Information See Attached Wire Instructions Sponsorship Benefits for Presenting Partner Sponsor: * Main Stage named after your brand * 4 10x10 on site vendor booths * 50 VIP Sponsor Passes / 50 GA tickets for both days * 4 Parking passes * Opportunity to participate in after party * Banner placement in venue (10) * Approved audio/video assets to be provided as promotional use for Herbo * Name and phrase called out on stage between performers set * Your logo and a link from our website to your website * Your logo on video wall * Your company name and logo as a presenting sponsor * Banner at main entrance of venue * On stage banner placement * Logo in Backstage/VIP area * Mention on social media * Logo on Step and Repeat * Logo on all promotional print Terms and Termination: The term of this agreement will begin on April 1, 2018 and continue until April 30, 2018 at 11:59pm.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original agreement regarding the payment schedule. The original text states payment 'to be paid in full upon signing of this agreement,' while the changed text indicates payment 'to be paid in 30 days after signing of this agreement.' This is a direct inconsistency, fitting the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, as it provides conflicting deadlines for payment within the same document and the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Terms and Termination: The term of this agreement will begin on April 1, 2018 and continue until April 30, 2018 at 11:59pm.
perturbation.changed_text: Terms and Termination: The term of this agreement will begin on April 1, 2018 and continue until May 30, 2018 at 11:59pm. However, the Promoter reserves the right to terminate this agreement at any time without prior notice.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates a conflict by introducing a contradictory termination clause. The original text specified a fixed term ending on April 30, 2018. The added clause grants the Promoter the right to terminate the agreement 'at any time without prior notice,' effectively negating the fixed term and making the agreement's duration uncertain. This directly contradicts the initial term agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Terms and Termination: The term of this agreement will begin on April 1, 2018 and continue until May 30, 2018 at 11:59pm. However, the Promoter reserves the right to terminate this agreement at any time without prior notice. Relationship of Parties: The parties are independent contractors with respect to one another. Nothing in this Agreement shall create any association, joint venture, partnership or agency relationship of any kind between the parties. 1 Source: ECO SCIENCE SOLUTIONS, INC., 8-K, 4/6/2018 Intellectual Property: Fruit of Life Productions LLC is the sole owner of all right, title and interest to all Kaya Fest information including Logo, tag lines, (Education before Recreation), Trademarks, trade names and copyrighted information. Sponsor agrees that it will not use Kaya Fest property in a manner that states or implies that Kaya Fest endorses Sponsor (or Sponsors products or services) without written approval from Fruit of Life Productions LLC. Idemnification: Sponsor shall indemnify and hold harmless, Fruit of Life Productions LLC, its related entities, partners, agents, officers, directors, employees, attorneys, heirs, successors, and assigns from against any and all claims, losses, damages, judgments, settlements, costs and expenses (including reasonable attorney's fees and expenses), and liabilities of every kind incurred as a result of: (i) any act or omission by Sponsor or its officers, directors, entities, employees, agents; (ii) any use of Sponsor's name, logo, Website, or other information, products, or service provided by Sponsor; and/or (iii) the inaccuracy or breach of any of the covenants, representations and warranties made by Sponsor in this Agreement. (iv) any changes in company value or brand value. The attendance and marketing reach estimates made in negotiations were made for the purposes of this agreement are mere estimate and not be interpreted as guaranties.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a fixed term ending on April 30, 2018. The changed text extends the term to May 30, 2018, but also grants the Promoter the right to terminate the agreement 'at any time without prior notice.' This creates an in-text contradiction because the added termination clause negates the fixed term, making the agreement's duration uncertain and unenforceable as initially stated. The contradicted text reflects this change, resulting in an internal contradiction because the contract is now self-conflicting.

perturbation.original_text: Assignment: This Agreement, or the rights granted under it, may not be assigned transferred or sublicense by either party without the express prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Assignment: This Agreement, or the rights granted under it, may not be assigned transferred or sublicense by the Sponsor without the express prior written consent of the Promoter. The Promoter may assign, transfer or sublicense this agreement without the need for Sponsor consent.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction and bias by creating unequal assignment rights. Initially, the agreement required express written consent from both parties for assignment, transfer, or sublicense. The modification allows the Promoter to assign, transfer, or sublicense the agreement without the Sponsor's consent, while the Sponsor still requires the Promoter's consent. This creates an imbalance in contractual obligations, favoring the Promoter and contradicting the initial mutual agreement of assignment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Assignment: This Agreement, or the rights granted under it, may not be assigned transferred or sublicense by the Sponsor without the express prior written consent of the Promoter. The Promoter may assign, transfer or sublicense this agreement without the need for Sponsor consent. Entire Agreement: This Agreement and its attachments constitute the entire agreement between the parties and supersede all prior agreements, oral or written, relating to the Sponsorship. This Agreement may only be admitted in a writing signed by both parties. The agreement is confidential, and the parties agree not to file or record in public records. Notice: All notices given under this Agreement shall be in writing, addressed to the parties at the addresses set forth below, and shall be deemed to have the duly given when delivered when sent by overnight courier, or certified mail (return receipt requested). Fruit of Life Productions LLC (Promoter) Address: 16115 SW 117t h Ave. Suite 21-A Miami, Florida 33177 EcoScience Solutions, Inc. (Sponsor) Address: 1135 Makawao Avenue, Suite 103-188 Makawao, Hawaii 96768 The Sponsor agrees that upon acceptance, this agreement shall be deemed to form and binding contract between the Sponsor and Promoter. The Sponsor agrees to abide by the terms set forth in the Terms and Conditions of Sponsorship agreement. All parties have executed this Agreement through their duly authorized representatives as of the first date written below. Sponsor: Eco Science Solutions, Inc. By: /s/Jeffery Taylor Name: Jeffery Taylor Title: CEO Date: 4/01/2018 Promoter: Fruit of Life Productions LLC: By:/s/Stella McLaughlan Name: Stella McLaughlan Title: Event Coordinator Date: 4/01/2018 3 Source: ECO SCIENCE SOLUTIONS, INC., 8-K, 4/6/2018
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates an in-text contradiction. The original text stated that neither party could assign, transfer, or sublicense without the other's consent. The changed text now allows the Promoter to do so without the Sponsor's consent, while the Sponsor still requires the Promoter's consent. The 'Contradicted Text' highlights the entire agreement and signing by both parties, which implies mutual agreement and equal rights, directly conflicting with the new unequal assignment rights granted to the Promoter. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.
